major_risks: Forecasting and impact-predictor/cost-modeling components are real but not strongly validated enough to headline as major contributions without softer wording.
Reviewer-facing wording still needs continued discipline around networking realism, QoS interpretation, and real-world applicability.
The checked-in artifact and SQLite footprint remains heavy even though the reviewer path is now clearly labeled.

pass_partial_fail_items.reason: Real simulator stack implemented across greennet/simulator.py, greennet/env.py, greennet/topology.py, greennet/traffic.py, and greennet/routing.py.
AI is real, but it performs link-state control over a fixed routing baseline; the evaluated policy is hybridized.
Power/carbon models, sleep logic, transition costs, and energy-aware evaluation are implemented.
React UI is real and buildable; Streamlit dashboard also exists.
all_on, heuristic, and ppo are compared in the official acceptance matrix and final report.
Methodology is documented and the critical artifact/claim drift was repaired, but some non-core supporting claims still need softer framing.
The repo explicitly reports that the original AI-energy hypothesis was not achieved.
Present, but stronger than the current forecasting/cost-model evidence supports.
Real unit and integration tests exist.
A canonical command exists, the final story is now cleaner, and the API state leak was fixed; remaining risk is mainly heavy artifact footprint rather than broken flow.
Acceptance matrix and final report pipeline are implemented.
FastAPI and React integration are real.
PPO exists and is trainable, but the final evaluated policy is hybridized and does not beat the strongest heuristic.
Forecasting is implemented but lightweight and weakly validated as a distinct contribution.
Impact predictor exists but should be framed as exploratory.
Core architecture is sound and the critical PPO/reproducibility issues were repaired, but realism framing and some evaluation interpretation caveats still matter.
Docs are now materially more honest, but overclaim risk still remains around realism, QoS interpretation, and exploratory side-components.
Canonical docs and the new submission index are good, but the repository is still large and historically layered.

checklist_items.item: Make the final ppo claim honest everywhere.
checklist_items.what_is_wrong: The evaluated ppo path is hybridized with rule-based override logic.
checklist_items.why_it_matters: This is the largest claim-honesty risk.
checklist_items.done_looks_like: Completed in this repair pass: reviewer-facing language now matches the actual evaluated controller while preserving the stable ppo policy id for compatibility.

Completed in this repair pass: the canonical default output path and reviewer docs now point to artifacts/final_pipeline/official_acceptance_v1/, while latest/ and official_matrix_v6/ are explicitly archival.

checklist_items.item: Fix /api/final_evaluation state leakage and the failing integration test.
checklist_items.what_is_wrong: API behavior is influenced by ambient SQLite state.
checklist_items.done_looks_like: Completed in this repair pass: DB-backed final-evaluation payloads are now validated against the current REPO_ROOT before they override repo-local artifacts, and the targeted API integration tests pass.

checklist_items.item: Resolve documented-vs-actual PPO artifact lineage mismatch.
checklist_items.what_is_wrong: Docs and checkpoint metadata disagree on the canonical PPO recipe.
checklist_items.done_looks_like: Completed in this repair pass: reviewer docs now match the checked-in checkpoint metadata, and train_official_ppo.json is explicitly labeled as an alternate longer-run recipe rather than the source of the current canonical family.

checklist_items.what_is_wrong: pytest and frontend lint both failed.
checklist_items.done_looks_like: Completed in this repair pass: pytest, lint, and frontend build all pass.

checklist_items.what_is_wrong: Duplicate exports, historical bundles, backups, caches, and bulk artifacts clutter the repo.
checklist_items.done_looks_like: Completed in this repair pass: a root submission index now defines the reviewer path, and the highest-confusion directories are clearly labeled in place as archival/internal rather than competing with the canonical bundle.
